ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
libffi:
- '3.5'
libxml2_devel:
- '2.14'
rust_compiler:
- rust
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
zlib:
- '1'
zstd:
- '1.5'

about.description:
Inko is a language for building concurrent software with confidence.
Inko makes it easy to build concurrent software, without having to
worry about unpredictable performance, unexpected runtime errors, or
race conditions.

Inko features deterministic automatic memory management, move
semantics, static typing, type-safe concurrency, efficient error
handling, and more. Inko source code is compiled to machine code
using LLVM.
about.summary: A language for building concurrent software with confidence

script.content:
$env.LLVM_SYS_180_PREFIX = $env.PREFIX

# The patched Config::new() resolves INKO_STD / INKO_RT at runtime
# from current_exe(); the compile-time env!() fallback still has to
# resolve to something, so we bake placeholder-free literals that
# rattler's prefix rewriter will leave alone.
$env.INKO_STD = "/nonexistent/inko/std"
$env.INKO_RT  = "/nonexistent/inko/runtime"

cargo auditable build --release --locked

# Conda-forge's Rust toolchain sets CARGO_BUILD_TARGET, so artifacts
# land in target/<triple>/release/. Fall back to target/release/ for
# native-only builds.
let triple = $env.CARGO_BUILD_TARGET? | default ''
let target_dir = if ($"target/($triple)/release/inko" | path exists) {
  $"target/($triple)/release"
} else {
  "target/release"
}

let install_rt  = $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/runtime"
mkdir $"($env.PREFIX)/bin"
mkdir $install_rt
cp $"($target_dir)/inko"      $"($env.PREFIX)/bin/inko"
cp $"($target_dir)/libinko.a" $"($install_rt)/libinko.a"

# The standard library ships as Inko source files; copy std/src
# wholesale and rename it to std so the installed layout matches
# lib/inko/std/... instead of lib/inko/src/....
cp -r std/src $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/src"
mv $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/src" $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/std"

cargo-bundle-licenses --format yaml --output THIRDPARTY.yml

script: echo 'type async Main { fn async main { } }' > hello.inko

raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: "0.20.0"

package:
  name: inko
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/inko-lang/inko/archive/refs/tags/v${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 19cef883272556d136d6866be6b96e6a45dafe414e8d3bfcadafc1ad3192b02b
  patches:
    - patches/0001-locate-std-and-runtime-relative-to-executable.patch

build:
  number: 0
  skip: win
  script:
    interpreter: nu
    env:
      CARGO_PROFILE_RELEASE_STRIP: symbols
    content: |
      $env.LLVM_SYS_180_PREFIX = $env.PREFIX

      # The patched Config::new() resolves INKO_STD / INKO_RT at runtime
      # from current_exe(); the compile-time env!() fallback still has to
      # resolve to something, so we bake placeholder-free literals that
      # rattler's prefix rewriter will leave alone.
      $env.INKO_STD = "/nonexistent/inko/std"
      $env.INKO_RT  = "/nonexistent/inko/runtime"

      cargo auditable build --release --locked

      # Conda-forge's Rust toolchain sets CARGO_BUILD_TARGET, so artifacts
      # land in target/<triple>/release/. Fall back to target/release/ for
      # native-only builds.
      let triple = $env.CARGO_BUILD_TARGET? | default ''
      let target_dir = if ($"target/($triple)/release/inko" | path exists) {
        $"target/($triple)/release"
      } else {
        "target/release"
      }

      let install_rt  = $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/runtime"
      mkdir $"($env.PREFIX)/bin"
      mkdir $install_rt
      cp $"($target_dir)/inko"      $"($env.PREFIX)/bin/inko"
      cp $"($target_dir)/libinko.a" $"($install_rt)/libinko.a"

      # The standard library ships as Inko source files; copy std/src
      # wholesale and rename it to std so the installed layout matches
      # lib/inko/std/... instead of lib/inko/src/....
      cp -r std/src $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/src"
      mv $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/src" $"($env.PREFIX)/lib/inko/std"

      cargo-bundle-licenses --format yaml --output THIRDPARTY.yml

requirements:
  build:
    - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('c') }}
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - ${{ compiler('rust') }}
    - cargo-bundle-licenses
    - cargo-auditable
    - nushell
  host:
    # inko's compiler crate hard-pins inkwell to its `llvm18-0` feature,
    # which is generated against LLVM 18 headers and is not forward-
    # compatible with later major versions. Bump this in lockstep with
    # any upstream inkwell feature-flag bump.
    - llvmdev 18.1.*
    # `zlib` (not `libzlib`) provides the `libz.so` symlink and headers
    # needed at link time. `libzlib` ships only the versioned runtime.
    - zlib
    - zstd
    # libxml2-devel provides the unversioned `libxml2.so` / `libxml2.dylib`
    # linker symlink and headers that llvm-sys needs — llvm-sys forwards
    # `-lxml2` to the final link because llvmdev was built with LLVM's
    # XML remark support. libxml2-devel pulls in libxml2-16 (the runtime)
    # as a hard dependency.
    - libxml2-devel
    - libffi

tests:
  - script:
      - inko --help
      - inko --version
      - echo 'type async Main { fn async main { } }' > hello.inko
      - inko check hello.inko
  - package_contents:
      bin:
        - inko
      files:
        - lib/inko/runtime/libinko.a

about:
  homepage: https://inko-lang.org
  summary: A language for building concurrent software with confidence
  description: |
    Inko is a language for building concurrent software with confidence.
    Inko makes it easy to build concurrent software, without having to
    worry about unpredictable performance, unexpected runtime errors, or
    race conditions.

    Inko features deterministic automatic memory management, move
    semantics, static typing, type-safe concurrency, efficient error
    handling, and more. Inko source code is compiled to machine code
    using LLVM.
  license: MPL-2.0
  license_file:
    - LICENSE
    - THIRDPARTY.yml
  documentation: https://docs.inko-lang.org
  repository: https://github.com/inko-lang/inko

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - tdejager
